sections.label: Section 1.910
sections.original: ἅ θʼ Ἕλλανα λιποῦσα δόμον
sections.translation: that left her home in Hellas , and sailed here with her Phrygian lover , bringing death to you , my dearest , for the sake of Troy , and emptying countless cities of their brave heroes .

sections.label: Section 1.915
sections.original: ἦ πολλὰ μὲν ζῶν , πολλὰ δʼ εἰς Ἅιδου μολών ,
sections.translation: Many indeed are the wounds , Thamyris , son of Philammon , that you have inflicted on my heart , in your life and in your death . Yes , for it was your pride , your own undoing , and your rivalry with the Muses that made me mother of this poor son of mine . For as I crossed the river ’ s streams

sections.label: Section 1.920
sections.original: λέκτροις ἐπλάθην Στρυμόνος φυταλμίοις ,
sections.translation: I came too near to Strymon ’ s fruitful couch , that day we Muses came to the brow of Mount Pangaeus with its soil of gold , furnished forth with all our music for one great trial of minstrel skill with that clever Thracian bard ; and we blinded him ,

sections.label: Section 1.925
sections.original: Θάμυριν , ὃς ἡμῶν πόλλʼ ἐδέννασεν τέχνην .
sections.translation: Thamyris , the man who often reviled our craft .

sections.label: Section 1.926
sections.original: κἀπεὶ σὲ τίκτω , συγγόνους αἰδουμένη
sections.translation: And then , when I gave birth to you , because I felt shame of my sisters and my virginity , I sent you to the swirling stream of your father , the river ; and Strymon did not entrust your nurture to mortal hands , but to the fountain nymphs .

sections.label: Section 1.930
sections.original: ἔνθʼ ἐκτραφεὶς κάλλιστα Παρθένων ὕπο ,
sections.translation: There you were reared most fairly by the maiden nymphs , and you ruled over Thrace , a leader among men , my child . So long as you ranged your native land in quest of bloody deeds of prowess I did not fear for your death ; but I forbade you to set out for Troy ,

sections.label: Section 1.935
sections.original: εἰδυῖα τὸν σὸν πότμον · ἀλλά σʼ Ἕκτορος
sections.translation: in my knowledge of your doom ; but Hector ’ s sages and those countless embassies persuaded you to go and help your friends .

sections.label: Section 1.938
sections.original: καὶ τοῦδʼ , Ἀθάνα , παντὸς αἰτία μόρου ,
sections.translation: This was your doing , Athena ; you alone are to blame for his death — neither Odysseus nor the son of Tydeus

sections.label: Section 1.940
sections.original: ἔδρασε δράσας — μὴ δόκει λεληθέναι .
sections.translation: had anything to do with it — do not think it has escaped my eye . And yet we sister Muses do special honor to your city , your land we chiefly haunt ; and those dark mysteries with their torch processions were revealed by Orpheus , cousin of this dead man

sections.label: Section 1.945
sections.original: τοῦδʼ ὃν κατακτείνεις σύ · Μουσαῖόν τε , σὸν
sections.translation: whom you have slain . Musaeus , too , your holy citizen , of all men most advanced in lore , was trained by Phoebus and us , the nine sisters . And here is your reward for this ; in my arms I hold my child and mourn for him . I ’ ll bring to you no other learned man .

sections.label: Section 1.950
sections.original: μάτην ἄρʼ ἡμᾶς Θρῄκιος τροχηλάτης
sections.translation: Vainly it seems the Thracian charioteer reviled us with plotting this man ’ s murder , Hector .

sections.label: Section 1.952
sections.original: ᾔδη τάδʼ · οὐδὲν μάντεων ἔδει φράσαι
sections.translation: I knew it ; it needed no seer to say that he had perished by the arts of Odysseus . Now I , when I saw the Hellene army camped

sections.label: Section 1.955
sections.original: λεύσσων , τί μὴν ἔμελλον οὐ πέμψειν φίλοις
sections.translation: in my land , of course would not hesitate to send heralds to my friends , bidding them come and help my country ; and so I sent , and he as in duty bound came to share my toils . I do not at all rejoice to see him dead . And now I am ready to raise a tomb for him

sections.label: Section 1.960
sections.original: καὶ ξυμπυρῶσαι μυρίων πέπλων χλιδήν ·
sections.translation: and burn at his pyre great store of fine raiment ; for he came as a friend and in sorrow he is going away .

sections.label: Section 1.962
sections.original: οὐκ εἶσι γαίας ἐς μελάγχιμον πέδον ·
sections.translation: He shall not go into earth ’ s dark soil ; so earnest a prayer will I address to the bride of the nether world , the daughter of the goddess Demeter , giver of increase ,

sections.label: Section 1.965
sections.original: ψυχὴν ἀνεῖναι τοῦδʼ · ὀφειλέτις δέ μοι
sections.translation: to release his soul ; and , debtor as she is to me , show that she honors the friends of Orpheus . And to me for the rest of time he will be as one who is dead and does not see the light ; for never again will he meet me or see his mother ;

sections.label: Section 1.970
sections.original: κρυπτὸς δʼ ἐν ἄντροις τῆς ὑπαργύρου χθονὸς
sections.translation: but he will lie hidden in a cavern of the land with veins of silver , restored to life , a deified man , just as the prophet of Bacchus dwelt in a grotto beneath Pangaeus , a god whom his votaries honored .

sections.label: Section 1.974
sections.original: ῥᾷον δὲ πένθος τῆς θαλασσίας θεοῦ
sections.translation: Lightly now shall I feel the grief of the sea-goddess ,

sections.label: Section 1.975
sections.original: οἴσω · θανεῖν γὰρ καὶ τὸν ἐκ κείνης χρεών .
sections.translation: for her son too must die . First then for you we sisters must chant our dirge , and then for Achilles when Thetis mourns some day . Pallas , your slayer , shall not save him ; so true the shaft Loxias keeps in his quiver for him .

sections.label: Section 1.980
sections.original: ὦ παιδοποιοὶ συμφοραί , πόνοι βροτῶν ·
sections.translation: Oh , the sorrows that a mother feels ! the troubles of mortals ! whoever fairly reckons you up will live and die childless and will have no children to bury .

sections.label: Section 1.983
sections.original: οὗτος μὲν ἤδη μητρὶ κηδεύειν μέλει ·
sections.translation: His mother now must see to this man ’ s burial ; but for you , Hector , if you will carry out any scheme ,

sections.label: Section 1.985
sections.original: Ἕκτορ , πάρεστι · φῶς γὰρ ἡμέρας τόδε .
sections.translation: now is the time , for day is dawning .

sections.label: Section 1.986
sections.original: χωρεῖτε , συμμάχους δʼ ὁπλίζεσθαι τάχος
sections.translation: Go , bid our allies arm at once and yoke the horses ; torch in hand you must await the blast of the Etrurian trumpet ; for I hope to pass beyond their army

sections.label: Section 1.990
sections.original: τείχη τʼ Ἀχαιῶν ναυσὶν αἶθον ἐμβαλεῖν
sections.translation: and walls and fire the ships of the Achaeans , bringing to the Trojans this approaching beam of the sun as the day of freedom .

sections.label: Section 993
sections.original: πείθου βασιλεῖ · στείχωμεν ὅπλοις
sections.translation: Obedience to our prince ! let us go to array ourselves in armor and tell these orders

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: τάδε φράζωμεν · τάχα δʼ ἂν νίκην
sections.translation: to our allies ; perhaps the god who is on our side may grant us victory .
